_note: MOCK procedure result. No real patient. For PreChart development/eval.

procedure.name: EUS-FNA of pancreatic head mass with same-session ERCP and biliary stent placement

code.display: Endoscopic ultrasonography with fine needle aspiration (procedure)

procedure.indication: 3.2 cm pancreatic head mass with painless obstructive jaundice (total bilirubin 4.1) and CA 19-9 890; tissue diagnosis and biliary decompression
procedure.sedation: Monitored anesthesia care (MAC) with propofol, anesthesiology-administered; prone/semi-prone positioning for ERCP
procedure.extent_reached: Second portion of duodenum; pancreatic head fully interrogated by linear echoendoscope; major papilla cannulated at ERCP
procedure.periprocedural_notes: Metformin 500 mg BID held morning of procedure per NPO plan. Documented prior rash to iodinated IV contrast — premedicated with methylprednisolone and diphenhydramine per protocol; low-osmolar contrast used sparingly under fluoroscopy with no reaction. OTC fish oil (omega-3) continued; not a clinically significant anticoagulant and did not require holding. No therapeutic anticoagulant/antiplatelet on file.

procedure_report:
PROCEDURE: Linear EUS with fine needle aspiration of pancreatic head mass, followed by same-session ERCP with biliary stent placement. INDICATION: pancreatic head mass with obstructive jaundice; tissue diagnosis and decompression. SEDATION: MAC (propofol). CONTRAST ALLERGY: prior rash to iodinated contrast — premedicated (steroid + antihistamine); minimal low-osmolar contrast used without reaction.

EUS FINDINGS: A hypoechoic, ill-defined mass measuring approximately 3.3 x 3.0 cm was identified in the pancreatic head. The mass abutted the portal confluence with preserved fat plane; no definite vascular invasion of the SMA/SMV was seen on this exam. Upstream main pancreatic duct and common bile duct were dilated (double-duct sign). A rounded 9 mm peripancreatic lymph node was noted. No ascites. Liver segments surveyed showed no discrete lesion. FNA was performed with a 22-gauge needle under Doppler guidance, 4 passes, with rapid on-site evaluation (ROSE) reporting atypical/malignant epithelial cells consistent with adenocarcinoma; additional passes collected for cell block and cytology.

ERCP FINDINGS: The major papilla was identified and cannulated selectively. Cholangiogram (limited contrast) demonstrated a tight ~2 cm distal common bile duct stricture at the level of the mass with marked upstream intra- and extrahepatic biliary dilation. A guidewire was advanced across the stricture. A 10 Fr x 9 cm plastic biliary stent was deployed across the stricture with brisk drainage of bile and contrast. Given the malignant appearance, metal stenting was deferred pending confirmed cytology and staging/resectability determination at tumor board. No sphincterotomy complication; prophylactic rectal indomethacin administered.

IMPRESSION: (1) Pancreatic head mass, ROSE positive for malignant cells — cytology/cell block pending. (2) Malignant-appearing distal CBD stricture, decompressed with plastic biliary stent — technically successful. (3) Suspicious 9 mm peripancreatic lymph node. Await final cytology and staging for MDT review.

findings.finding: Hypoechoic ill-defined pancreatic head mass, ~3.3 x 3.0 cm, abutting portal confluence without definite vascular invasion
findings.action_taken: EUS-FNA, 22G, 4 passes; ROSE positive for malignant cells

findings.finding: Distal common bile duct stricture (~2 cm) with upstream biliary dilation (double-duct sign)
findings.action_taken: guidewire crossing and 10 Fr x 9 cm plastic biliary stent placement with good drainage

pathology.diagnosis: ROSE: positive for malignant cells, favor adenocarcinoma; final cytology and cell block (including immunostains) pending

disposition: Discharged home same day in stable condition with escort after MAC recovery. Return precautions for post-ERCP pancreatitis (worsening abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting) reviewed. Biliary stent care and expected improvement in jaundice discussed.

followup.recommendation: Review final EUS-FNA cytology/cell block; ROSE already positive for malignant cells — communicate confirmed result to patient and update chart from 'suspected' to tissue-confirmed once final
Present at multidisciplinary pancreatic tumor board for staging and resectability; obtain pancreatic-protocol CT/MRI if not already staged
Medical/surgical oncology referral for treatment planning once cytology finalized
Monitor bilirubin trend post-stent to confirm biliary decompression; plan for metal stent exchange if unresectable/neoadjuvant pathway
Resume metformin 500 mg BID after procedure once tolerating oral intake; monitor for post-ERCP pancreatitis
Follow up 9 mm peripancreatic lymph node in staging workup

planted_followup_items.description: EUS-FNA cytology and cell block pending; ROSE already positive for malignant cells consistent with pancreatic adenocarcinoma. Final result must be communicated and the chart diagnosis (currently 'suspected/provisional') updated to tissue-confirmed.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: call patient with final cytology; document tissue confirmation; trigger oncology pathway

planted_followup_items.description: Positive malignant cytology on ROSE for a pancreatic head mass — a critical result requiring expedited oncology/tumor board handoff and staging.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: present at MDT/tumor board; refer to oncology; complete staging imaging

planted_followup_items.description: Medical/surgical oncology referral for treatment planning (resectable vs neoadjuvant vs palliative).
planted_followup_items.expected_action: place oncology referral once cytology finalized

planted_followup_items.description: Plastic biliary stent placed; likely stent exchange to self-expanding metal stent depending on resectability/neoadjuvant plan, and plastic stents require exchange to avoid occlusion/cholangitis.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: schedule ERCP stent exchange per oncologic plan or if signs of occlusion

planted_followup_items.description: Post-ERCP pancreatitis watch after biliary cannulation/stenting; prophylactic rectal indomethacin given.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: monitor for abdominal pain/vomiting; instruct patient on return precautions

planted_followup_items.description: Metformin 500 mg BID held on procedure day for NPO; not an anticoagulant/antiplatelet. Resume once tolerating oral intake, with attention to post-procedure pancreatitis risk.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: confirm safe metformin resumption timing with patient/PCP

planted_followup_items.description: Bilirubin trend must be rechecked post-stent to confirm biliary decompression is effective.
planted_followup_items.expected_action: recheck LFTs/bilirubin in 1-2 weeks; escalate if not improving

planted_followup_items.description: 9 mm rounded peripancreatic lymph node noted on EUS; a suspicious pending-workup lesion relevant to nodal staging of the pancreatic malignancy.

post_procedure_followup._note: Patient-reported stream after the procedure — the inbound signal the LeftBehind product triages. Each contact is labeled ground truth so triage can be scored.

contacts.verbatim: My throat is a bit scratchy and I feel kind of bloated and burpy since the procedure.
contacts.clinical_signal: Expected post-endoscopy sore throat and insufflation-related bloating/gas after EUS/ERCP; no abdominal pain, fever, or vomiting reported
contacts.expected_action: Reassure; scratchy throat and bloating/gas are expected after upper endoscopy and self-limited within 24-48h. Reinforce post-ERCP return precautions (worsening abdominal pain, nausea/vomiting, fever).
contacts.rationale: Common benign post-endoscopy symptoms with no features of post-ERCP pancreatitis or cholangitis; no escalation needed.

contacts.verbatim: When am I supposed to start my metformin again, and is it okay to keep taking my fish oil?
contacts.clinical_signal: Patient asking about resuming metformin (held on procedure day for NPO) and continuing OTC fish oil
contacts.expected_action: Answer from the plan: resume metformin 500 mg BID once tolerating oral intake (about 24-48h) if no abdominal pain or vomiting; fish oil is not a true blood thinner and may be continued.
contacts.rationale: Directly answerable from the medication-reconciliation follow-up item; no clinical red flag.

contacts.verbatim: My eyes and skin look less yellow and my urine is lighter — is that a good sign? And when will I hear about the biopsy results?
contacts.clinical_signal: Improving jaundice consistent with successful biliary stent decompression; inquiry about pending EUS-FNA cytology
contacts.expected_action: Reassure that lessening jaundice and lighter urine indicate the stent is draining as intended; explain final cytology is pending (expected ~2026-07-24) and the team will call with results and next steps; a follow-up bilirubin check is planned.
contacts.rationale: Answerable from the procedure result and follow-up plan (pending pathology and post-stent bilirubin monitoring); improving jaundice is the expected benign course, not a red flag.
